topic.what: SPECA: specification-anchored security audit framework deriving typed security properties from natural-language specs to drive structured proof-attempt auditing
topic.how: 6-phase pipeline: spec discovery, subgraph extraction, property generation (STRIDE+CWE), code pre-resolution, property-grounded audit (map-prove-stress), severity-preserving review with 3-gate false-positive filtering
topic.why_matters: Code-level tools lack representational vocabulary for specification-governed vulnerabilities; SPECA enables spec-dependent detection, cross-implementation comparison, and interpretable false positives traceable to pipeline phases

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of H/M/L contest vulnerabilities recovered
characteristics.evidence: Sherlock Ethereum Fusaka Audit Contest with 366 auditor submissions and expert adjudication
characteristics.context: Expert-augmented configuration; 4 additional novel bugs confirmed by developer fix commits including KZG cryptographic invariant violation missed by all 366 auditors

characteristics.vs_baseline: 88.9% precision on RepoAudit benchmark vs baseline match
characteristics.evidence: Sherlock benchmark post-review (N=72 findings); RepoAudit benchmark comparison
characteristics.context: Broad precision counts all non-FP labels; strict counts only H/M/L-matched findings; 24 confirmed FPs from 72 post-review findings

characteristics.what_it_measures: Performance difference between automated-only and expert-augmented property generation
characteristics.evidence: Ablation comparing 6-phase automated pipeline vs expert-injected properties
characteristics.context: Gap identifies cryptographic invariants and protocol-lifecycle rules as frontier for automated property generation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Ability to hold property vocabulary constant while comparing 10 implementations
characteristics.value: Yes - shared vocabulary enables controlled comparison
characteristics.vs_baseline: No prior tool provides this capability
characteristics.evidence: 10 Ethereum client implementations compared under shared property vocabulary
characteristics.context: Phases 1-3 execute once producing shared property vocabulary; phases 4-6 execute independently per implementation

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of FPs traceable to specific pipeline phases with actionable improvement targets
characteristics.value: 100% - 3 recurring causes: trust boundary misunderstanding (50%), code reading errors (37.5%), specification misinterpretation (12.5%)
characteristics.vs_baseline: No prior tool provides structured FP taxonomy with phase-origin tracing
characteristics.evidence: Analysis of 16 representative false positives on RepoAudit benchmark
characteristics.context: Each root cause traceable to specific pipeline phase; yields ranked actionable improvement targets

characteristics.what_it_measures: Relationship between model capability and property-faithful auditing behavior
characteristics.value: Non-monotonic: Sonnet 4.5 (88.9% precision, 12 candidates), Sonnet 4 (81.2% precision, 18 candidates), DeepSeek R1 (72.7% precision, 7 candidates)
characteristics.vs_baseline: Property adherence effect: more capable models audit more faithfully within stated property scope
characteristics.evidence: Multi-model sensitivity analysis on RepoAudit C/C++ benchmark
characteristics.context: Property generation quality becomes binding constraint as models improve; reduces serendipitous beyond-property discoveries

characteristics.evidence: Cost breakdown across model configurations and benchmarks
characteristics.context: Multi-model analysis cost $93-101 per model; evaluation scripts and artifacts released publicly
